title: Medical emergency
short_description: Immediate medical danger. 911 first, then protect clients and notify leadership.
do_now: Call 911 immediately.
Provide first aid / CPR if trained and needed until EMS arrives.
Clear other clients from the area and send them to rooms.
Keep client as comfortable and safe as possible until EMS arrives.
follow_up: If resident requests ER rather than obvious 911 event, contact Leah Scott RN via Teams.
Take recovery statement sheet and MAR when applicable.
Send staff email and document all actions taken.
special_notes: If first responders are called, leadership notification is required.
When in doubt, call the shift supervisor or on-call supervisor.

title: Medical non-emergency
short_description: Non-serious medical issue. Usually document and route to normal workflow.
do_now: Assess for signs that this is actually emergent.
Encourage client to complete an HSR form.
Provide normal non-emergency support and monitor.
follow_up: Escalate only if symptoms worsen or no longer appear non-serious.
special_notes: Examples in source: toothache, headache, backache, body aches, requesting medication change.
No call required for non-serious injury.

title: Medication error
short_description: Medication error requires immediate supervisor guidance and CIR completion.
do_now: Call the shift supervisor or on-call supervisor immediately.
Follow supervisor instructions exactly.
Monitor the client for changes while guidance is being arranged.
documentation: Client chart / EVOLV note if applicable
follow_up: Record what happened, what was observed, and what supervisor instructions were given.
special_notes: This is not a 'wait until later' item.

title: Client behavioral / emotional crisis
short_description: Behavioral or emotional crisis requiring clinical on-call guidance.
do_now: Stabilize the environment and stay present with the client.
Call clinical on-call.
Follow on-call recommendations exactly.
documentation: CIR if incident threshold is met
follow_up: Clinical on-call may recommend in-person response, higher level of care, or monitoring plan.
special_notes: Called 'On-Call Therapist' in source; normalized to Clinical On Call in UI.

title: Client returning from hospital
short_description: Standard return-from-hospital workflow. No immediate on-call call required.
do_now: File or scan discharge paperwork.
Get medication brought in by client to medical ASAP.
If medical is unavailable, place medication in designated secure location.
Assist client returning to room / milieu.
follow_up: Use admin-configured secure medication drop-off location rather than person-specific desk instruction.
special_notes: Original source contains outdated person-specific desk instruction; keep that in admin review only.

title: Client wanting to leave
short_description: Client is expressing intent to leave; route through peer support on-call.
do_now: Inform client of protocol to talk with staff before leaving.
Call peer support on-call.
If client declines intervention and leaves, have them sign ASA paper when possible.
follow_up: Peer support on-call may consult clinical on-call if needed.

title: Client leaves ASA / AWOL
short_description: Client has already left. Supervisor-on-call action is required.
do_now: Call the on-call supervisor immediately.
Secure known facts: time last seen, destination if known, attempts to contact, and any safety concerns.
follow_up: Notify DCBS if client has an open CPS case.
special_notes: Do not wait to see if the client returns.

title: Allegation of sexual abuse or harassment
short_description: High-sensitivity allegation requiring immediate manager notification and confidentiality.
do_now: Ensure immediate safety and privacy.
Call RST Manager / Program Manager immediately.
Limit sharing to those who must know.
follow_up: Follow internal abuse / reporting workflow based on leadership guidance.
special_notes: Treat as confidential and high sensitivity.

title: Verbal aggression
short_description: Verbal aggression may require supervisor involvement and documentation.
do_now: De-escalate and create space.
If a shift supervisor is on duty, contact them.
If no supervisor is working, call supervisor on-call.
follow_up: Supervisor determines next steps and whether manager should be looped in.
special_notes: If risk escalates toward physical violence, move to physical aggression / 911 workflow.

title: Physical aggression
do_now: Call 911 immediately.
Dismiss all clients from the area to rooms until further notice.
Protect staff and clients without escalating the conflict.
follow_up: Leadership notification required because first responders were called.

title: Allegation of physical abuse / assault
short_description: Assault allegation requires immediate safety assessment, possible 911, and fact-gathering workflow.
do_now: Assess whether 911 is needed right now.
Call shift supervisor; if no shift supervisor is working, call on-call supervisor.
Gather statements and facts without leading witnesses.
follow_up: Start at the beginning and build a timeline to the last event.
Use each person's own words when possible.
special_notes: It is not staff's job to determine true or false on the spot; document what is presented.

title: Safety threat
short_description: Dangerous safety threat requiring police / first responders when needed.
do_now: Call 911 if police or first responders are needed.
Protect clients and staff and clear the immediate area as needed.
Once first responders are called, notify shift supervisor or RST Manager.
follow_up: Leadership notification is required when first responders are called.
special_notes: Examples: weapon, bodily fluids, dangerous liquid, trespassing, threat to self or others.

title: Fire
short_description: Fire workflow with 911 first, then supervisor, then RACE/PASS instructions.
do_now: Call 911.
After calling 911, call shift supervisor or supervisor on-call even if a small fire was put out.
Use RACE: Remove, Activate alarm/911, Confine, Evacuate.
Use PASS for extinguisher only on a small manageable fire.
special_notes: Use ABC extinguisher if appropriate and safe.
Leadership notice is required because first responders were called.

title: Client late from curfew / pass / hospital return
short_description: If client is over 30 minutes late, supervisor involvement is required.
do_now: Call the client's phone if available.
Call emergency contact listed in EVOLV.
If more than 30 minutes late, call shift supervisor or on-call supervisor.
follow_up: Supervisor follows up with RST Manager regarding possible AWOL determination.
special_notes: Do not wait past the 30-minute threshold.
